architecture_summary: Register messaging and phone adapter capability ids in the gateway dispatcher using the same signed worker pattern as browser, document, voice, and email/calendar. The dispatcher adds messaging and phone payload builders that carry tenant, capability, action, connector, approval, participant, and command metadata to signed workers. Platform worker resolution expands the AdapterWorkerClients tuple to include messaging and phone clients from bundles, direct attributes, nested runtime attributes, or environment builders. Existing AdapterExternalEffectEvidence assessment remains the admission boundary for external-write SMS, chat, and phone actions before succeeded dispatch is accepted.
data_model_changes: extend dispatcher-local adapter worker client resolution from browser, document, voice, and email/calendar to include messaging and phone client slots
add messaging dispatch payload fields for connector_id, recipients, body, thread_id, query, approval_id, and merged execution metadata
add phone dispatch payload fields for connector_id, call_id, callees, callers, transcript, approval_id, and merged execution metadata
rollback_plan: Remove messaging and phone dispatcher registration, payload builders, expanded platform worker client resolution, default registration exports, focused dispatcher tests, and SDLC evidence artifacts, then rerun adapter dispatcher tests, worker contract tests, SDLC artifact validation, security review validation, release readiness validation, and workspace governance preflight.
migration_plan: No persistence, schema, deployment, or worker endpoint migration is required. Existing configured messaging and phone worker clients become reachable through governed dispatch only when the platform or environment provides signed worker endpoints. Missing clients continue to fail closed.
